threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the tube' instead of just 'the overground' like the rest of us. They'll know you're fresh off the train.
Spotted a bloke in a Hawaiian shirt arguing with a pigeon outside the Ritzy at 2am, claiming it owed him money. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Realized I was home when a guy in flip-flops tried to haggle over a bus ticket with the BVG inspector at Hermannplatz.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a late-night waffle truck parked outside a techno club, serving fresh waffles with ketchup. It's a vibe, trust me.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local when they complain about the 'new' taco truck on 24th refusing to serve al pastor after 10 PM.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tutu dancing on the bus as it crawled past the Civic Center. Only in SF do delays inspire performances like that.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists keep asking for gluten-free 'pa de pessic' but miss the joy of a local 'coca de recapte'—try the simple joys!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers by their reluctance to step off the sidewalk when a local is approaching. They still think space is shareable.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn't stop for you because you didn’t raise your hand. It's a rite of passage.
Last night on the 36, a guy was rapping about lost socks. The bus driver kept nodding like it was a private concert. Oddly riveting.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The time I saw a pop-up café inside the old bus depot on Karl-Marx-Straße, packed with locals and laughter. It felt like a real community moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you witness a spontaneous dance party at a U-Bahn station during a transit strike—turns the wait into a mini festival.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from SF when they complain about BART delays as if it’s a personal affront, not just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Today, the Muni driver stopped to let a guy in a hot dog costume do a quick jig at Civic Center before moving on. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all bars here close early, but some keep serving until dawn, especially near the Pl. del Nord. Just not the ones in guidebooks.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones taking selfies by every fountain, blissfully unaware that locals avoid them like the Monday morning train.
